examples.title: Peterson's Algorithm for Two Processes
examples.description: Software solution for mutual exclusion between two processes
examples.explanation: Each process sets its flag to indicate desire to enter, then yields to the other process. It waits in the while loop until the other process is not ready or it's its turn. This satisfies mutual exclusion, progress, and bounded waiting.

examples.title: Semaphore Implementation (wait and signal)
examples.description: Basic semaphore operations showing how they coordinate processes
examples.explanation: wait() decrements the semaphore value and blocks the process if result is negative. signal() increments the value and wakes a waiting process if the value is still negative. This ensures proper synchronization.

examples.title: Test-and-Set Lock Implementation
examples.description: Hardware atomic instruction for implementing mutual exclusion
examples.explanation: testAndSet atomically returns the old value while setting the lock to true. If lock was false, we get false and enter CS. If lock was true, we get true and continue waiting. This is a spinlock - wastes CPU but works without sleep/wakeup overhead.

examples.title: Producer-Consumer with Semaphores
examples.description: Classic bounded buffer problem solved using semaphores
examples.explanation: empty tracks available slots, full tracks filled slots, mutex protects buffer access. Producer waits for space, adds item, signals fullness. Consumer waits for item, removes it, signals emptyness. This prevents buffer overflow and underflow.

examples.title: Monitor Example - Account Withdrawal
examples.explanation: The monitor ensures only one thread can execute any procedure at a time. The 'while' loop with condition variable allows thread to sleep while waiting for sufficient balance. When deposit() is called, it signals waiting withdrawers. This prevents race conditions on balance automatically.
